title: heracleum dissectum subsp. moellendorffii
intro: Heracleum dissectum subsp. moellendorffii, also known as the Korean angelica or Dong quai, is a plant species in the carrot family that is native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Heracleum dissectum subsp. moellendorffii include reducing menstrual cramps, improving circulation, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including menopausal symptoms and digestive issues.
